reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary: reviewer noted that the code should also decrement ->readers and check if it's zero, similar to another part of the codebase, potentially fixing a bug introduced by the patch
reviews.raw_body:
Hmm..  The other place where we decrement ->readers we then check if it
is zero and if CACHE_PENDING is clear - and do something.
I suspect we should do that here.
This bug (if I'm right and it is a bug) if there before you patch, but
now might be a good time to fix it?

Thanks.  Nice cleanups.

NeilBrown

2026-02-22.patch_summary: This patch series improves the scalability of the sunrpc cache infrastructure by converting a global spinlock and waitqueue to per-cache-detail locks and waitqueues, and splitting the cache detail queue into two separate lists for requests and readers.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged a missing patch to address NeilBrown's feedback and plans to add it before the existing patches

Author acknowledged that there were issues with the spinlocks in the patch, agreed to fix them in version 2.
sentiment_signals: plans to add a new patch
reviews.raw_body:
Good catch. I'll add a patch to fix that preceding these patches, so it
can go to stable (if we think that's worthwhile).

---

Thanks for the review! I'll send a v2 with the spinlock fixes and the
above bugfix.

2026-02-23.patch_summary: This patch series improves the scalability of the sunrpc cache infrastructure by converting a global spinlock and waitqueue to per-cache-detail locks and waitqueues, and splitting the cache detail queue into two separate lists for requests and readers.
